classes.label: Country
classes.description: Sovereign country identified by database code.

classes.label: Province
classes.description: First-level administrative division within a country.

classes.label: City
classes.description: City identified relative to province and country.

classes.label: Border
classes.description: Border segment between two countries with a length attribute.

classes.label: Country Continent Inclusion
classes.description: Reified membership of a country in a continent with percentage.

notes: Schema profiler reports zero declared foreign keys, so mappings rely on primary-key structure, naming conventions, and composite key alignment.
Connector tables with meaningful attributes were generally modeled as reified classes: borders, encompasses, Spoken, Religion, EthnicGroup, and population history tables.
No inverse object properties were added to remain conservative.
Tables mentioned by tooling but not present in parsed definitions (for example isMember, geo_Source, mergesWith, located, locatedOn, islandIn) were not mapped.
uncertain_areas: Province.CapProv likely references the province part of the capital city key; provinceCapitalCity mapping is plausible but slightly less certain than direct countryCapitalCity.
Politics.Dependent looks like a country-code reference, but because semantics are underspecified and a paired textual WasDependent column exists, no object property was added for it.
River.River, River.Lake, and River.Sea encode alternative downstream targets under a check constraint; separate object properties were used instead of a single polymorphic target property.

Natural-feature columns such as Mountain.Mountains, Island.Islands, Lake.River, and River.Mountains appear to reference named ranges or groups, but without supporting tables or constraints they were not promoted to object properties.
